name: Mzansi Mix Vol. 2
description: General logic and reasoning with a South African flavour — taxi culture, braai, load shedding and everyday SA life
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: A loaf of bread has 20 slices. If you use 2 slices per sandwich, how many sandwiches can you make?
questions.explanation: Shame, 20 slices divided by 2 per sandwich = 10 sandwiches. That's a lekker packed lunch!

questions.question: If load shedding starts at 6pm and lasts 2 hours, what time does the power come back on?
questions.explanation: 6pm plus 2 hours = 8pm. Although knowing Eskom, it might be a bit longer hey!

questions.question: Bongani has 3 sisters and each sister has 1 brother. How many children are in the family?
questions.explanation: Bongani IS the one brother that each sister has! So it's 3 sisters + 1 brother (Bongani) = 4 kids. Don't let this one catch you!

questions.question: A taxi fare costs R15. If 4 friends share the cost equally, how many rand does each person pay?
questions.explanation: Trick question! Each person pays R15 — a taxi fare is R15 per person, not total. That's how taxis work, my friend!

questions.question: If you're facing north and turn 180 degrees, which direction are you now facing?
questions.explanation: 180 degrees is a half turn, so you go from north straight to south. Easy peasy!

questions.question: Nomsa buys 3 bunny chows at R45 each and pays with R200. How much change does she get?
questions.explanation: 3 bunny chows at R45 each = R135. R200 minus R135 = R65 change. Still enough for a cold drink to wash it down!

questions.question: A taxi leaves Bree Street every 15 minutes starting at 06:00. What time does the 5th taxi leave?
questions.explanation: The 1st taxi leaves at 06:00, 2nd at 06:15, 3rd at 06:30, 4th at 06:45, 5th at 07:00. Count carefully!

questions.question: A braai pack has 12 boerewors rolls. If each person eats 2 rolls, how many people can eat?
questions.explanation: 12 rolls divided by 2 per person = 6 people sorted. Lekker, everyone gets fed!

questions.question: If the Rand is at R18 to $1, how many Rand do you need for $5?
questions.explanation: R18 times 5 = R90. Eish, the exchange rate is hectic these days!

questions.question: Sipho is 5th in line at Home Affairs. 3 people leave and 2 more join behind him. What position is he now?
questions.explanation: If 3 people ahead of Sipho leave, he moves from 5th to 2nd. The people joining behind don't affect his position. Still a long wait at Home Affairs though!

questions.question: During load shedding Stage 2, your area loses power for 2 hours, 3 times a day. How many hours of power do you have in a day?
questions.explanation: 3 slots of 2 hours = 6 hours without power. 24 minus 6 = 18 hours with power. Still not lekker, but it could be worse!

questions.question: A Gautrain trip from Sandton to OR Tambo costs R185. If you take this trip twice a week for 4 weeks, how much do you spend in Rand?
questions.explanation: R185 per trip x 2 trips per week x 4 weeks = R1,480. That's a lot of moolah for commuting!

questions.question: A minibus taxi fits 15 passengers. If the taxi rank has 4 full taxis and 1 taxi that's half full, how many passengers are there in total?
questions.explanation: 4 full taxis = 4 x 15 = 60. Half of 15 is 7.5, but you can't have half a person, so it's 7 (we round down). 60 + 7 = 67 passengers. Taxi maths!

questions.question: Thabo buys 2 six-packs of Castle Lager. He gives 3 beers to his neighbour and drinks 2 himself. How many are left?
questions.explanation: 2 six-packs = 12 beers. Minus 3 for the neighbour, minus 2 for himself = 12 - 5 = 7 beers left. Sharing is caring!

questions.question: Put these South African cities in order from NORTH to SOUTH: (1) Cape Town, (2) Durban, (3) Polokwane, (4) Johannesburg
questions.explanation: From north to south: Polokwane (Limpopo), Johannesburg (Gauteng), Durban (KZN), Cape Town (Western Cape). Know your geography, boet!

questions.question: Load shedding is on a 4-day cycle. If your area has load shedding at 18:00 today (Monday), on which day will it next be at 18:00?
questions.explanation: A 4-day cycle means the same time slot repeats every 4 days. Monday + 4 days = Friday. Now you can plan your braai!

questions.question: A taxi driver makes 8 trips a day. Each trip earns R180 in fares. He spends R320 on petrol daily. What's his daily profit in Rand?
questions.explanation: 8 trips x R180 = R1,440 in fares. R1,440 minus R320 petrol = R1,120 profit. Not bad for a day's hustling!

questions.question: You have a 5-litre bucket and a 3-litre bucket. How many steps does it take to measure exactly 4 litres?
questions.explanation: Fill the 5L, pour into 3L (leaves 2L in the 5L), empty the 3L, pour the 2L into the 3L, fill the 5L again — that's 5 steps to get exactly 4L in the 5L bucket. Classic water puzzle with a braai twist!

questions.question: In a taxi, the front row seats 2 passengers. The next 3 rows each seat 4 passengers. And the back row seats 3 passengers. How many passengers fit in total?
questions.explanation: Front: 2, middle rows: 3 x 4 = 12, back: 3. Total: 2 + 12 + 3 = 17 passengers. A full taxi is a happy taxi!

questions.question: If a potjie recipe needs 2.5 kg of meat and you're cooking for a group that's 3 times the normal size, how many kg of meat do you need?
questions.explanation: 2.5 kg times 3 = 7.5 kg of meat. That's a serious potjie! Better get the big pot.

questions.question: A farmer has chickens and goats. He counts 20 heads and 56 legs. How many goats does he have?
questions.explanation: If all 20 animals were chickens, that's 40 legs. We have 56 - 40 = 16 extra legs. Each goat adds 2 extra legs over a chicken, so 16 / 2 = 8 goats. Classic algebra on the farm!

questions.question: A Soweto derby match has 80,000 fans. If 60% support Chiefs and the rest support Pirates, how many more Chiefs fans are there than Pirates fans?
questions.explanation: 60% of 80,000 = 48,000 Chiefs fans. 40% = 32,000 Pirates fans. 48,000 - 32,000 = 16,000 more Chiefs fans. Amakhosi!

questions.question: You're driving from Joburg to Durban (about 570 km). Your car uses 8 litres per 100 km and petrol costs R25 per litre. About how much will the petrol cost?
questions.explanation: 570 km at 8L/100km = 45.6 litres. 45.6 x R25 = R1,140. Eish, road trips aren't cheap anymore!

questions.question: A braai grid has 8 boerewors coils on it. You flip each one exactly once. After flipping, you remove every second coil (2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th). How many coils remain on the grid?
questions.explanation: You remove the 2nd, 4th, 6th, and 8th coils — that's 4 removed. 8 minus 4 = 4 coils left. The flipping is a red herring!

questions.question: Put these Rand amounts in order from LEAST to MOST: (1) Six R20 notes, (2) Three R50 notes, (3) Thirteen R10 notes, (4) One R200 note
questions.explanation: Six R20s = R120, Three R50s = R150, Thirteen R10s = R130, One R200 = R200. Order: R120, R130, R150, R200. You gotta do the maths before you sort!

questions.question: A township barbershop charges R40 for a haircut. On Saturday, the barber does 18 cuts. He pays R120 rent, R80 for electricity, and R50 for supplies. What's his profit for the day?
questions.explanation: 18 cuts x R40 = R720 revenue. Expenses: R120 + R80 + R50 = R250. Profit: R720 - R250 = R470. The barber's doing alright for a Saturday, ne!

questions.question: A clock gains 5 minutes every hour. If you set it correctly at 12:00, what time will it show after 3 real hours have passed? Answer in format like 1515 for 3:15pm.
questions.explanation: After 3 real hours, the clock gains 3 x 5 = 15 extra minutes. So it shows 3:15pm (15:15 or 1515). That clock needs fixing, just like Eskom's schedule!

questions.question: In a room of 5 people, everyone shakes hands with everyone else exactly once. How many handshakes happen?
questions.explanation: The formula is n(n-1)/2. So 5 x 4 / 2 = 10 handshakes. Each of 5 people shakes 4 others' hands, but we divide by 2 because each handshake involves 2 people!

questions.question: A taxi does 3 round trips between Sandton and Alexandra daily. The one-way distance is 8 km. If the taxi costs R2.50 per km to run, what's the daily running cost?
questions.explanation: 3 round trips = 3 x 2 x 8 km = 48 km total. 48 x R2.50 = R120. That's the cost of keeping Mzansi moving!

questions.question: You're tiling a stoep that's 4m x 3m using tiles that are 50cm x 50cm. How many tiles do you need?
questions.explanation: 4m = 8 tiles across, 3m = 6 tiles down. 8 x 6 = 48 tiles. Now that's a lekker stoep to sit on with your coffee!

questions.question: A spaza shop buys cooldrinks at R8 each and sells them at R12 each. To make R500 profit, how many cooldrinks must they sell?
questions.explanation: Profit per cooldrink = R12 - R8 = R4. R500 / R4 = 125 cooldrinks. That's a lot of Fanta and Coke!

questions.question: Three friends — Ayanda, Busi, and Craig — each cooked a different dish: potjie, braai, and bobotie. Ayanda didn't braai. Craig didn't make potjie or braai. What did Ayanda cook?
questions.explanation: Craig didn't make potjie or braai, so Craig made bobotie. Ayanda didn't braai, so Ayanda made potjie. That leaves Busi on the braai. Logic, baby!

questions.question: A Joburg to Cape Town flight takes 2 hours. The plane left at 14:30 but was delayed 45 minutes on the runway. When does it land?
questions.explanation: Departed at 14:30, sat on runway for 45 min (takes off at 15:15), then 2 hours flying = lands at 17:15. SA time is the same across the country, so no time zone tricks!

questions.question: A painter can paint a room in 6 hours. His assistant can paint the same room in 12 hours. Working together, how many hours will it take them? Give your answer as a whole number (round down).
questions.explanation: Painter does 1/6 per hour, assistant does 1/12 per hour. Together: 1/6 + 1/12 = 3/12 = 1/4 per hour. So it takes 4 hours. Teamwork makes the dream work!

questions.question: Put these events in the order they happened: (1) 2010 FIFA World Cup in SA, (2) Nelson Mandela released from prison, (3) End of load shedding Stage 6, (4) South Africa became a democracy
questions.explanation: Mandela released (1990), democracy (1994), World Cup (2010), end of Stage 6 load shedding (2024). What a journey, Mzansi!

questions.question: In a 15-a-side rugby match, if every player on one team passes the ball to every other player on their team exactly once, how many total passes happen?
questions.explanation: Using n(n-1)/2: 15 x 14 / 2 = 105 passes. That's a lot of passes before you even get to the try line!

questions.question: A solar panel generates 4 kWh per day. A geyser uses 10 kWh per day. Load shedding cuts power for 6 hours a day (you lose 25% of grid power). If grid power is 30 kWh/day normally, what's your total available energy?
questions.explanation: Grid power with load shedding: 30 x 0.75 = 22.5 kWh. Plus solar: 22.5 + 4 = 26.5 kWh. That's why everyone's going solar, boet!

questions.question: A minibus taxi makes a profit of R150 per trip. The driver gets 35%, the owner gets 50%, and 15% goes to maintenance. If the taxi does 6 trips a day, how much does the driver earn daily?
questions.explanation: Total daily profit: 6 x R150 = R900. Driver gets 35% of R900 = R315. Not bad for a day behind the wheel!

questions.question: You want to enclose a rectangular garden with 24 metres of fencing. Which dimensions give you the MAXIMUM area?
questions.explanation: For a fixed perimeter, a square gives the maximum area. 6m x 6m = 36m² (perimeter = 24m). Compare: 8x4 = 32m², 10x2 = 20m², 7x5 = 35m². The square wins!

questions.question: A Joburg Uber charges R10 base fare plus R7.50 per km. A Bolt charges R8 base fare plus R8 per km. At how many kilometres does the cost become the same?
questions.explanation: Set them equal: 10 + 7.5k = 8 + 8k. So 2 = 0.5k, meaning k = 4 km. After 4 km they cost the same. Under 4 km, Bolt is cheaper!

questions.question: A chessboard has 64 squares. If you place 1 grain of rice on the first square, 2 on the second, 4 on the third, doubling each time, how many grains are on the 10th square?
questions.explanation: Each square has 2^(n-1) grains. The 10th square has 2^9 = 512 grains. And by the 64th square? More rice than exists on Earth! That's exponential growth for you.

questions.question: A train leaves Joburg for Pretoria at 80 km/h. At the same time, a train leaves Pretoria for Joburg at 60 km/h. The distance is 56 km. How far from Joburg do they meet?
questions.explanation: Combined speed: 80 + 60 = 140 km/h. Time to meet: 56/140 = 0.4 hours. Joburg train travels: 80 x 0.4 = 32 km from Joburg. Choo choo!

questions.question: At a Durban July horse race, 12 horses run. In how many different ways can the top 3 positions (win, place, show) be filled?
questions.explanation: This is a permutation: 12 x 11 x 10 = 1,320 possible outcomes. That's why betting is so hard — the odds are stacked!

questions.question: Three taps fill a swimming pool. Tap A fills it in 10 hours, Tap B in 15 hours, and Tap C in 30 hours. With all three open, how many hours to fill the pool?
questions.explanation: Rates: 1/10 + 1/15 + 1/30 = 3/30 + 2/30 + 1/30 = 6/30 = 1/5 per hour. So it takes 5 hours. Pool party in 5 hours, everyone!

questions.question: Put these in order from SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) 3/7, (2) 5/12, (3) 7/15, (4) 2/5
The number 142857 is special. What do you get when you multiply it by 7?
questions.explanation: 142857 x 7 = 999,999. This is the famous cyclic number! 1/7 = 0.142857142857... and it creates magic when multiplied. Maths is lekker!

questions.question: In a braai competition, 8 teams play a round-robin (everyone plays everyone once). Each match takes 45 minutes and only one braai can happen at a time. What's the minimum time needed to complete all matches?
questions.explanation: Number of matches in a round-robin: 8 x 7 / 2 = 28 matches. 28 x 45 minutes = 1,260 minutes = 21 hours. That's one epic braai-off!

questions.question: You have 12 identical-looking R5 coins, but one is slightly lighter (counterfeit). Using a balance scale, what's the MINIMUM number of weighings needed to guarantee finding the fake coin?
questions.explanation: Split into 3 groups of 4. Weigh group 1 vs group 2. If equal, the fake is in group 3. If unequal, take the lighter group. Now split 4 into 1,1,2. Weigh the two singles. 3 weighings max! Classic balance puzzle.

questions.question: A taxi route has 8 stops. A ticket is needed for each unique pair of stops (boarding at one, alighting at another). How many different types of tickets must be printed?
questions.explanation: Since boarding at Stop A and alighting at Stop B is different from the reverse, this is a permutation: 8 x 7 = 56 different tickets. That's a lot of ticket types for one route!

questions.question: A biltong maker dries meat and it loses 80% of its weight (water evaporating). If he starts with 100 kg of raw meat, how many kg of raw meat does he need to produce 50 kg of biltong?
questions.explanation: If meat loses 80%, you keep 20%. So raw weight x 0.20 = biltong weight. For 50 kg biltong: 50 / 0.20 = 250 kg raw meat. That's a LOT of beef!

questions.question: A group of friends sit in a circle at a braai. Each person shakes hands with everyone EXCEPT the two people sitting directly next to them (and themselves). If there are 10 people, how many handshakes happen?
questions.explanation: Total possible handshakes: C(10,2) = 45. Minus the 10 adjacent pairs in a circle = 35 handshakes. Or: each person shakes with 7 others, so 10 x 7 / 2 = 35. Eish, circle maths is no joke!

questions.question: You have 3 identical boxes. One has 2 gold Krugerrands, one has 2 silver coins, one has 1 of each. All labels are WRONG. You pull one coin from the box labelled 'Mixed'. It's gold. What's in the box labelled 'Silver'?
questions.explanation: Since ALL labels are wrong, the 'Mixed' box can't be mixed. You drew gold, so it must be the 2-gold box. The 'Silver' label is also wrong, so it's not silver. The 'Gold' label is wrong, so it's not gold — it must be silver. That leaves 'Silver' box = mixed (1 gold, 1 silver). Big brain time!

questions.question: In a township, houses are numbered 1 to 200. How many times does the digit '1' appear in all the house numbers combined?
questions.explanation: Count 1s in units place: 1,11,21,...,191 = 20 times. In tens place: 10-19, 110-119, 210-... but only up to 200, so 10-19 (10 times) and 110-119 (10 times) = 20 times. In hundreds place: 100-199 = 100 times. Total: 20 + 20 + 100 = 140. Eish, counting digits is no joke!

questions.question: A Joburg traffic light cycle is: Green 40s, Yellow 5s, Red 35s. If you arrive at a random time, what's the probability you'll wait MORE than 20 seconds before it turns green?
questions.explanation: Total cycle = 80s. During green (40s) you don't wait at all. During yellow (5s) you wait 35s down to 30s — all >20s, so 5s count. During red (35s), your wait goes from 35s down to 0s — you wait >20s only in the first 15s of red. Favourable time: 5 + 15 = 20s out of 80s. Probability = 20/80 = 1/4. Only 1% of people can work through traffic light probability at a braai!

questions.question: Arrange these South African events from EARLIEST to LATEST: (1) First democratic election, (2) Discovery of gold on the Witwatersrand, (3) Sharpeville massacre, (4) Arrival of Jan van Riebeeck
options: Discovery of gold on the Witwatersrand
questions.explanation: Jan van Riebeeck arrived in 1652, gold discovered on the Witwatersrand in 1886, Sharpeville massacre in 1960, first democratic election in 1994. Only a true history buff gets this one right!
